title: F1-panel: replace config.ts placeholder with clack TUI loop driven by schema introspection
description: Replace the rc.15 placeholder body of `configCmd.run` with a clack-based interactive panel: `intro` → menu loop (`select` field name → display current value → `text` or `select` to edit → atomic write to fabric.config.json → re-render menu, with explicit `exit` choice) → `outro`. Drive the menu from `getPanelFields()` (TASK-005). KEEP the `installMcpClients` named export unchanged (re-imported by install.ts). Top-level CLI flag set: only `--target`. Uninit workspace path (fabric.config.json absent) → render error message + hint to run `fab install`, do NOT auto-bootstrap. Atomic write strategy: write to `fabric.config.json.tmp` then rename — reuse `atomicWriteJson` from `@fenglimg/fabric-shared/node/atomic-write` (already imported in install.ts). NO lock check (no fab serve contention). All user-facing strings in this command MUST honor `fabric_language` via `t()` from `../i18n.js` — add the new i18n keys (`cli.config.intro`, `cli.config.menu.title`, `cli.config.fields.<key>.label`, `cli.config.fields.<key>.description`, `cli.config.exit.choice`, `cli.config.write.success`, `cli.config.write.failure`, `cli.config.uninit.error`, `cli.config.uninit.hint`, `cli.config.invalid.input`) to the i18n string tables.

files.change: Replace placeholder console.log with: (1) check fabric.config.json exists → if not, log error + hint, exit 1; (2) `intro(t('cli.config.intro'))`; (3) menu loop: `select` over getPanelFields() entries + 'exit' choice → if exit, break; (4) for selected field: display current value, prompt edit via `text`+validate or `select`+enum_values; (5) on accept: re-load config, mutate field, atomicWriteJson; (6) re-render menu; (7) `outro` on exit. Handle isCancel at every prompt → graceful cancel.
Confirm args block has ONLY `target` (already correct in placeholder); no flags added
MUST remain unchanged — install.ts re-imports installMcpClients via `import * as configCommand`

files.target: i18n string tables (locate via grep for existing 'cli.config.' keys)
files.change: Add new keys: cli.config.intro, cli.config.menu.title, cli.config.fields.<each-key>.label, cli.config.fields.<each-key>.description, cli.config.exit.choice, cli.config.write.success, cli.config.write.failure, cli.config.uninit.error, cli.config.uninit.hint, cli.config.invalid.input — for all supported locales (en, zh-CN, zh-CN-hybrid)

implementation: Locate i18n string-table files (grep for existing key 'cli.config.placeholder' to find files)
Add new i18n keys for all panel strings across all supported locales
Replace configCmd.run body: uninit-check → intro → menu loop → outro

Menu loop iteration: `select({ message, options: [...fields.map(f => ({ value: f.key, label: t(f.label_i18n_key) + ` (current: ${f.format_for_display(currentConfig[f.key])})` })), { value: '__exit__', label: t('cli.config.exit.choice') }] })`
Field edit: branch on f.widget — 'select' uses select with enum_values; 'text' uses text with `validate: (raw) => f.validate(raw).ok ? undefined : f.validate(raw).error`
Atomic write: re-read current fabric.config.json (avoid stomping on concurrent edits), spread + override field, call `atomicWriteJson(configPath, mergedConfig)`
Wrap each prompt with isCancel check → if cancelled, `cancel(t('cli.config.cancelled'))` + return
Verify `installMcpClients`, `loadFabricConfig`, `resolveServerPath`, type exports remain unchanged (anti-regression for install.ts consumer)

reference.pattern: packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts (clack imports + group/select/intro/outro flow + atomicWriteJson usage)
reference.examples: install.ts:1-37 shows the canonical clack import block + atomicWriteJson import — copy verbatim

criteria: File `packages/cli/src/commands/config.ts` no longer contains the literal `t('cli.config.placeholder')` console.log (replaced by full clack panel)
Named export `installMcpClients` still present and signature unchanged (verify via TypeScript: `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli build` passes)
Args block contains ONLY `target` — no other flags added
Running `fab config` in a workspace WITHOUT fabric.config.json prints an error message containing 'fab install' and exits non-zero
Running `fab config` in a workspace WITH fabric.config.json launches the interactive panel showing all 11 fields from getPanelFields()
After editing a field and confirming, `fabric.config.json` on disk reflects the new value AND a `fabric.config.json.tmp` file is NOT left behind (atomic write succeeded)
Test command: `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -- config` exits 0 (TASK-007 supplies the tests)
Behavioral expectation: a user running `fab config` interactively edits Group A/B/C fields with localized labels in their fabric_language without touching JSON manually

rationale.chosen_approach: Clack-based menu loop driven by schema introspection — matches the CLI design principle '能交互选的就别做 flag', leverages existing install.ts patterns, and keeps the field list a single-source-of-truth (getPanelFields)
alternatives_considered: Single `--field <key> --value <val>` flag form (rejected: violates interactive-first principle, requires users to know field names)
TUI dashboard with all fields visible at once (rejected: clack lacks this widget; would require ink/blessed; over-engineered for 11 fields)
Auto-bootstrap on uninit (rejected: violates clean-slate + drift→abort principle from feedback_cli_design.md)
decision_factors: Consistency with install.ts UX (same widget vocabulary)
Single source of truth: PanelFieldMeta (TASK-005) drives both rendering and validation
Atomic write avoids partial-write corruption on Ctrl-C mid-edit
rationale.tradeoffs: Menu loop is ~150 LOC vs ~10-line flag-based alternative; justified by the user-facing UX win and avoiding the doc burden of explaining 11 flags

manual_checks: Run `fab config` in a fresh test workspace without fabric.config.json — verify error message + 'fab install' hint
Run `fab config` after `fab install`, navigate to fabric_language, change to 'zh-CN', verify fabric.config.json on disk updated
Run `fab config`, edit review_hint_pending_count to '0', verify validation rejects (must be positive)
Run `fab config`, edit a field, then re-launch and verify the new value is shown in the menu (re-render works)
Verify `fab install` still wires MCP clients (anti-regression for installMcpClients export)
success_metrics: Zero `.tmp` files leftover after 100 simulated edits
